summary: Applied notification profiles on the live mail platform by provisioning dedicated operator, platform, and agent sender identities, binding each to a scoped mail-gateway API key, and verifying per-profile delivery through the managed gateway.

verification.observed: The live gateway catalog returned operator-alerts, platform-transactional, and agent-reports from http://127.0.0.1:8081/v1/profiles using the admin API key.
The operator-alerts profile key received HTTP 403 when it attempted to send with profile_id platform-transactional.
Live sends through the gateway returned HTTP 200 via the Brevo API for alerts@example.com, platform@example.com, and agents@example.com.
The gateway state recorded requests_total=1 and brevo_success_total=1 for each of operator-alerts, platform-transactional, and agent-reports after focused verification.

notes: The live apply used the repo-managed mail platform playbook with --limit docker-runtime-lv3,monitoring-lv3 because the DNS and host-ingress portions of the mail stack were already converged and HETZNER_DNS_API_TOKEN was not present in the controller shell.

The broader mail-platform verification playbook hit intermittent SSH transport failures to monitoring-lv3 and legacy inbound-probe timing issues unrelated to ADR 0050, so final acceptance used the focused notification-profile verification playbook on docker-runtime-lv3.
